source.reference: SciBench dataset, source: Engel & Reid 'Thermodynamics, Statistical Thermodynamics, & Kinetics', problem 1.5. Verified by manual calculation: n = PV/RT = 97500 * 2.30e-4 / (8.314 * 296.25) = 22.425/2463.0 = 0.009103 mol; M_avg = 0.3554/0.009103 = 39.04 g/mol; x_butane = (39.04 - 30.07)/(58.12 - 30.07) = 8.97/28.05 = 0.3198 ≈ 32.0%.

problem.statement: A gas sample is known to be a mixture of ethane and butane. A bulb having a 230.0 cm^3 capacity is filled with the gas to a pressure of 97.5 × 10^3 Pa at 23.1°C. If the mass of the gas in the bulb is 0.3554 g, what is the mole percent of butane in the mixture?
problem.task: Calculate the mole percent of butane in the gas mixture.

volume.description: Volume of the gas bulb (= 2.300e-4 m^3)

mass.description: Total mass of the gas sample

answer.description: Mole percent of butane in the ethane/butane mixture

answer._comment: Allow ±1% for rounding in molar masses and intermediate steps

must_include: Use the ideal gas law to find total moles of gas
Calculate the average molar mass from total mass and total moles
Set up a linear equation relating average molar mass to composition
must_not_include: Assume the gas is pure ethane or pure butane
Use mass percent instead of mole percent

domain_context.recommended_thermo_model: Ideal gas law (PV = nRT) — appropriate at low pressure (≈1 atm) for light hydrocarbons
key_assumptions: Ideal gas behavior at ~1 atm and 23°C
Only ethane and butane are present (binary mixture)
common_mistakes: Forgetting to convert cm^3 to m^3 (factor of 1e-6)
Confusing mole percent with mass percent
Using the wrong gas constant units (must be 8.314 J/(mol K) with Pa and m^3)
Algebraic error in the weighted average molar mass equation

reasoning_checkpoints.checkpoint: Converts units: cm^3 → m^3, °C → K for use in PV = nRT
Calculates total moles using ideal gas law: n = PV/RT
Calculates average molar mass: M_avg = total mass / total moles
Sets up linear mixing rule: M_avg = x_ethane * M_ethane + x_butane * M_butane with x_ethane + x_butane = 1
Solves for x_butane and converts to mole percent

mole_fraction_bounds._comment: Mole percent must be between 0 and 100

average_molar_mass_bounds._comment: Average molar mass must be between the two pure component values
